channel.title: US Top News and Analysis
channel.description: CNBC is the world leader in business news and real-time financial market coverage. Find fast, actionable information.

item.title: Warren Buffett on the market today: 'It's tough to find values when everybody is preferring gambling'
item.description: The investor has been sharply critical of the stock market, one that he says is increasingly defined by speculative trading as opposed to long-term investing.

item.title: Wholesale prices unexpectedly declined 0.3% in June on big drop in gasoline
item.description: As with consumer prices, the index benefited from easing energy costs, particularly as oil fell due to the brief pause in tensions between the U.S. and Iran.

item.title: New York Fed President Williams says inflation has peaked, rates 'well positioned'
item.description: Williams cited five reasons why he expects the latest price surge has run its course.

item.title: Stocks rise on Big Tech gains, encouraging inflation signs: Live updates
item.description: S&P 500 futures edged higher Wednesday, led by gains in chipmakers, after a rally fueled by softer-than-expected inflation data.

item.title: DTCC, Wall Street’s post-trade powerhouse, tests tokenized markets with industry heavy hitters
item.description: The DTCC is pushing to bring tokenization of real world assets from concept to reality using existing financial infrastructure.

item.title: Buy SpaceX shares ahead of this week's Starship launch, says UBS
item.description: UBS keeps its buy rating and sees SpaceX reaching huge milestones if its next test flight is successful.

item.title: ‘Arsenal of democracy’: Jamie Dimon announces $24 million effort to boost American shipbuilding
item.description: JPMorgan Chase CEO Jamie Dimon unveiled a $24 million package to bolster American shipbuilding, funding a new submarine facility at the Philadelphia Navy Yard.

item.title: Watch Fed Chairman Kevin Warsh testify live before Senate banking committee
item.description: Part of congressionally mandated Capitol Hill appearances for the central bank leader, Warsh spoke Tuesday to the House Financial Services Committee.

item.title: Goldman Sachs’ former top lawyer tells House Epstein was a ‘masterful liar’ who used her to bolster his standing
item.description: Kathryn Ruemmler said earlier this year she would leave Goldman Sachs at the end of June after fallout over her emails with Jeffrey Epstein.

item.title: Todd Blanche is asked 'how long you intend to put up with that Kash Patel character?'
item.description: Todd Blanche previously served as a criminal defense lawyer for President Donald Trump.

item.title: Mortgage rates rise to highest level in nearly a year, causing homebuyers to pause
item.description: Mortgage rates moved higher last week, causing buyers to pull back, but refinancing did see small gains.

item.title: 'Miracle on the Hudson' pilot has early-stage Alzheimer’s disease
item.description: C.B. "Sully" Sullenberger safely landed an Airbus A320 on New York's Hudson River in 2009 after hitting a flock of geese, saving all 155 on board.

item.title: U.S. agency to vote to end 39% local TV station ownership cap
item.description: The FCC in March approved the sale of ‌local station owner Tegna to Nexstar, which would expand Nexstar's presence to cover 80% of U.S. ​TV households.

item.title: U.S. launches fresh wave of strikes on Iran, as analysts warn conflict risks becoming 'forever war'
item.description: Fighting in the Middle East has intensified in recent days, but could escalate further if Iran fails to cooperate, President Donald Trump said.

item.title: Amazon shares are really cheap compared with competitors Walmart and Alphabet, says Jefferies
item.description: Amazon is looking less expensive than comparable stocks such as Walmart and Alphabet, making it an attractive addition to portfolios, per Jefferies.

item.title: Morgan Stanley posts record quarterly revenue and profit as equities trading surges 69%
item.description: Like at peers Goldman Sachs and JPMorgan Chase, a massive beat in equities trading drove the quarter's outsized results.

item.title: Stripe, Advent make $53 billion takeover offer for PayPal, sending stock soaring
item.description: Stripe and Advent International have made a joint offer to acquire PayPal for $60.50 per share, in a deal that would valued at more than $53 billion.

item.title: IRS chief Frank Bisignano will lead Trump Accounts expansion
item.description: The Treasury Department is putting a top official in charge of the new program as it enrolls millions of families.

item.title: Hassett sees no 'excuse' to raise rates, says Warsh will push Fed to 'right answer'
item.description: Hassett, on CNBC, credited President Donald Trump's policies after the latest consumer price index reading came in much lower than expected.

item.title: Star analyst Dan Ives forms Yorkville Ives merchant bank after leaving Wedbush
item.description: The new firm, Yorkville Ives & Co., will combine investment banking, equity research, institutional trading and principal investing.

item.title: Jim Cramer sees more upside for Goldman and addresses Wells Fargo's biggest question
item.description: The Investing Club holds its "Morning Meeting" every weekday at 10:20 a.m. ET.

item.title: Home prices hit a new record high — these 3 strategies can help you save
item.description: Choosing specific lenders and loan types, plus improving your credit, can help you save.

item.title: Warren Buffett calls Bill Gates' actions with Epstein 'distasteful' but says people make mistakes
item.description: The Berkshire Hathaway chairman left out the Gates Foundation from his annual charitable stock gifts for the first time in 20 years.

item.title: Americans say they need $1.2 million to retire comfortably, survey finds — but many expect to fall short
item.description: Just 30% of workplace retirement plan investors think they'll reach $1 million before retiring, a new survey finds. Here's what's getting in their way.

item.title: Alibaba’s U.S.-listed shares rise 4% after Qwen AI set to be integrated in Apple Intelligence
item.description: The Cyberspace Administration of China on Wednesday included Apple's AI services on a list of approved providers.

item.title: ASML shares fall after hiking sales forecast for second time this year on strong AI chip demand
item.description: ASML on Wednesday raised its guidance for a second time this year as its customers continue to ramp up their production capacity of AI chips.

item.title: Stocks making the biggest moves premarket: BlackRock, PayPal, ASML, Morgan Stanley & more
item.description: These are the stocks posting the largest moves premarket.

item.title: Bipartisan group of senators propose Social Security reform process ahead of funding shortfall
item.description: Social Security's trust fund that helps pay retirement benefits may run out in about six years. A bipartisan group of senators is calling for earlier reform.

item.title: Warsh pledges Fed policy 'regime change' to rid inflation 'tax' on American people
item.description: Warsh pledged Tuesday to "get monetary policy right" and defeat the inflation that has bedeviled the central bank for the past five years.

item.title: The AI boom just found two new winners: Goldman Sachs and JPMorgan Chase
item.description: Goldman Sachs and JPMorgan showed that Wall Street is a major beneficiary of the AI boom, with record revenue driven by surging trading and investment banking.
